description: ColE1/pMB1 Rop (Rom) protein: a 63-aa homodimer that enhances RNA I-RNA II pairing to repress primer formation, lowering plasmid copy number

references.authors: Cesareni G, Muesing MA, Polisky B
references.title: Control of ColE1 DNA replication: the rop gene product negatively affects transcription from the replication primer promoter
references.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A 79(20):6313-6317 (1982)

references.title: Control of ColE1 plasmid replication: enhancement of binding of RNA I to the primer transcript by the Rom protein

label: rop
note: ColE1/pMB1 Rop (Rom): 63-aa homodimeric four-helix bundle that enhances RNA I-RNA II pairing to repress primer formation and lower plasmid copy number

provenance.sequence_source: UniProt P03051 (Rop/Rom, ColE1/pMB1 copy-number repressor); the stored 63-aa sequence is a 100% match to the UniProt canonical (verified by tools/import_uniprot_features.py, identity 1.0). The rop gene lies just downstream of the ColE1/pMB1 replication origin (present e.g. in pBR322, GenBank J01749).

functional_claims.label: Rop (Rom) enhances binding of RNA I to the RNA II primer precursor, inhibiting primer formation and thereby repressing initiation of plasmid replication.

source.quote: The Rom protein, a plasmid-specified 63 amino acid protein, enhances the inhibitory effect of RNA I on primer formation by enhancing the binding of RNA I to RNA II.

functional_claims.label: Rop is a trans-acting repressor of the RNA primer promoter (its name = 'repressor of primer'), negatively regulating ColE1/pMB1 plasmid copy number.

source.quote: Transcription from the RNA primer promoter, however, is repressed by the product of a trans-acting plasmid gene product, which we have designated rop (for repressor of primer).
